spec.description: CI/CD observability for a Jenkins controller instrumented with the OpenTelemetry plugin. Covers build outcomes, agent pool health, executor and queue capacity, JVM internals, and per-build distributed traces for stage/step drill-down.

options.content:
## CI-CD / Jenkins Controller — architecture & how to read this board

This board is shaped to the **actual CI architecture** of this platform, not a generic Jenkins dashboard.

**The pipeline topology**
- A **seed job** (`seed-jobs`, Job DSL in `jenkins/pipelines/seed/`) generates one `pipelineJob` per service × environment: `gateway`, `gateway-develop`, `jhipstersamplemicroservice(-develop)`, `microservices-k6-smoke(-develop)`. There is **no multibranch / organization-folder / webhook scanning**.
- Each generated job calls the **declarative shared-library** pipeline `MicroservicesPipeline(...)` — stages: Checkout → Semgrep → CodeQL → Trivy → Build & Test → Build & Push image → Deploy (ArgoCD GitOps) → Smoke Test → k6.
- Builds run on **ephemeral Kubernetes pod agents** (kubernetes-plugin, one 9-container pod per build, `containerCap` 10–20). The controller's built-in node has **`numExecutors: 0`** — it never runs builds directly.
- Deploy is **GitOps**: the pipeline bumps the image tag in the gitops repo and **ArgoCD** syncs it; there is no `kubectl apply` from Jenkins.

**Metric families (Jenkins OpenTelemetry plugin, service `jenkins`)**
- **`ci_pipeline_run_*`** — OTel CI/CD run metrics: launched / started / active / completed / success / failed / aborted + run duration histogram. ⚠️ **These are controller-wide aggregates** — the `ci_pipeline_id` label is not usable for per-pipeline breakdown in this plugin version. To inspect an **individual build, stage, or step**, use the **Build traces** panel at the bottom.
- **`jenkins_*`** — controller internals: executors, build queue, ephemeral cloud agents, plugins.
- **`jvm_*`** — JVM runtime: heap, non-heap, GC pauses, threads, CPU utilization.

**Dashboard sections at a glance**

| Section | What it answers |
|---|---|
| **Controller health** | Is the controller up? What are the headline build numbers for this time window? |
| **Pipeline runs — aggregate** | How many builds ran, what is the success rate, how long do they take? |
| **Build outcomes** | Trend view of success rate, failure rate, and average duration over time. |
| **Ephemeral build agents & queue** | Are agent pods provisioning correctly? Is the build queue backing up? |
| **Controller JVM** | Is the Jenkins process healthy — any memory pressure or GC pauses? |
| **Logs & traces** | Per-build drill-down: traces show every pipeline/stage/step span; logs show controller output. |

**Why some panels read `0` — and why that's correct here**
- 🟢 **Healthy `0`** — *Builds failed / aborted*, *Agent launch failures*, *Queue* are `0` on a green, idle controller.
- ⚙️ **Ephemeral agents** — *Built-in executors* shows the controller's static count (`numExecutors: 0` here); actual builds run on ephemeral agent pods. Busy executor counts can exceed that during a build because each agent pod also contributes executors. Real capacity is set by `containerCap`, not executor count. The clearest throughput signals are **Active pipeline runs** and **Cloud agent pods completed**.
- 📈 **Needs a recent build** — rate and duration panels show flat/zero when no build ran in the selected time window. Widen the range or trigger a build.

spec.title: Build success rate
spec.description: Aggregate across all seed jobs, over the selected range.

spec.title: Builds completed
spec.description: Total builds that reached a terminal state (success, failed, or aborted) in the selected time range.

spec.title: Builds failed
spec.description: Builds that ended with a failure in the selected range. Zero is healthy on an idle or green controller.

spec.title: Builds aborted
spec.description: Builds manually stopped or timed out in the selected range. Persistent non-zero values may indicate flaky timeouts.

spec.title: Active runs now
spec.description: Pipeline runs currently in progress (live gauge, not a rate). Watch this to confirm a build has started.

spec.title: Agents online
spec.description: Connected agents (built-in node + any live ephemeral agent pods).

spec.title: Built-in executors
spec.description: Static executors on the controller's built-in node (numExecutors:0 means builds run on ephemeral agents, NOT here).

spec.title: Busy executors (all nodes)
spec.description: Busy executors across the controller and all ephemeral agent pods. Can exceed the built-in executor count because each agent pod contributes its own executors. Use 'Active runs now' for a cleaner concurrency signal.

spec.title: Queue size
spec.description: Jobs waiting for a free agent-pod slot (containerCap).

spec.title: Cloud agent pods (total)
spec.description: Ephemeral k8s agent pods provisioned+completed since controller start — ≈ total builds run.

spec.title: Plugins installed
spec.description: Total number of plugins loaded by the Jenkins controller.

spec.title: Plugin updates
spec.description: Plugins with available updates. Non-zero warrants scheduling a maintenance window.

spec.title: Run outcomes (builds per interval: success / failed / aborted)
spec.description: Build COUNTS per scrape interval (not per-second) — readable for a low-volume CI. Bars rise when builds finish.

spec.title: Run lifecycle (builds per interval: launched / started / completed)
spec.description: Launched = entered the system; started = agent connected and build began; completed = reached a terminal result. A persistent gap between launched and started reveals agent provisioning latency.

spec.title: Active runs over time
spec.description: Concurrent pipeline runs in progress at each scrape interval. Useful to confirm a build started or to spot concurrency saturation.

spec.title: Run duration percentiles (p50 / p95 / p99)
spec.description: Whole-pipeline run time distribution over the visible range (p50 / p95 / p99). Shows zero when no builds ran in the window — widen the time range or run a build. For per-stage timing, use the Build traces panel.

spec.title: Completed by result /s
spec.description: Completed runs grouped by result label on the duration metric.

spec.title: Build success rate %
spec.description: Rolling success rate over time. Sudden drops indicate a broken pipeline or an underlying infrastructure issue.

spec.title: Failed builds /s
spec.description: Rate of failed builds per second. Any sustained non-zero value warrants investigation.

spec.title: Avg run duration
spec.description: Mean wall-clock time per completed build. Spikes may indicate slow stages, resource contention, or a stuck build.

spec.title: Cloud agent pods completed /s
spec.description: Rate of ephemeral k8s agent pods finishing a build — the real build-throughput signal here.

spec.title: Agents online vs total
spec.description: Online = agents with active connections; total = all registered agents (online + offline). Persistent offline agents indicate provisioning or networking issues.

spec.title: Agent launch failures /s
spec.description: Pod-agent scheduling/connection failures. 0 = healthy.

spec.title: Executor states (busy / idle / available)
spec.description: Busy = actively running a build step; idle = connected but waiting; available = free executor slots. Busy exceeding the built-in count is expected — ephemeral agent pods add their own executors.

spec.title: Build queue states (blocked / buildable / waiting)
spec.description: Blocked = held by a lock or dependency; buildable = ready to run but no agent slot yet; waiting = throttled. A growing buildable count signals that agent capacity (containerCap) is exhausted.

spec.title: Avg queue wait time
spec.description: Mean time a job waited for a free agent-pod slot before starting.

spec.title: Queue throughput (jobs leaving /s)
spec.description: Rate at which jobs leave the build queue per second — a proxy for build throughput. Low values with a non-empty queue indicate a stuck executor or agent provisioning failure.

spec.title: Heap used
spec.description: Heap memory consumed by the Jenkins JVM. Approaching the heap limit causes GC pressure and controller slowdowns.

spec.title: GC pause p99
spec.description: 99th-percentile GC pause duration. Spikes above ~200 ms cause latency on the controller UI and API.

spec.title: Live threads
spec.description: Total live threads in the JVM. Steady growth over time may indicate a thread leak.

spec.title: JVM CPU utilization
spec.description: Recent CPU utilization of the Jenkins JVM process (0–1). Sustained values above 0.8 indicate overload.

spec.title: Non-heap used
spec.description: Non-heap memory used (metaspace + code cache). Steady growth typically indicates class loading or plugin accumulation.

spec.title: Build traces — pipeline / stage / step spans (the per-job view)
spec.description: Since the run metrics above are aggregate, THIS is where you inspect an individual build: each trace = one pipeline run, with stage and step spans (Checkout, Semgrep, Build & Test, Deploy, Smoke Test…). Click a Trace ID → trace view (links to logs).

spec.title: Jenkins controller logs
spec.description: Controller pod logs. Filter by $level. (Build-agent logs are on the ephemeral pods — see a build's console / its trace.)

spec.expr: {k8s_namespace_name="jenkins"} | detected_level=~"$level" | json | line_format "{{ if .message }}{{ .message }}{{ else if .msg }}{{ .msg }}{{ else }}{{ __line__ }}{{ end }}"

spec.title: Pipeline runs — aggregate (all seed jobs · ci_pipeline_run_*; per-build detail in Traces below)
Ephemeral build agents (Kubernetes) & queue — this architecture's capacity model
Controller JVM (summary — deep dive in 'CI-CD JVM internals')
CI-CD / Jenkins Controller
